evidence_summary: Tour guide shares ghost stories and chilling experiences from Port Arthur Historic Site The Port Arthur Historic Site ghost tour manager shares some chilling experiences visitors have reported. It's known as one of Australia's most haunted places, and every year 20,000 people brave a ghost tour at the Port Arthur Historic Site. Guiding manager Jake Bradshaw shares some stories with ABC Tasmania Afternoons presenter Joel Rheinberger, including the one that gets the biggest reaction from visitors. From pickaxe murders, the "Blue Lady" and the Parsonage, Mr Bradshaw says there's plenty of chilling stories from the old convict site. Joel Rheinberger How many people come to be spooked on ghost tours at Port Arthur? Jake Bradshaw Oh, lots. It's very.

evidence_summary: Murder on the Tasman Express: A radio play from ABC Radio Hobart. Episode 3 Can ghosts help our trapped travellers unmask the killer hiding right in front of them? The lights have gone out, the rain is coming down and the travellers in the Tasman Express are getting spooked. Are they truly alone? Can voices of radio broadcasts past shed light on who the murderer is? ABC Radio Tasmania Evenings with Helen Shield presents a six-part whodunnit murder mystery radio play, Murder on the Tasman Express. You can catch up on episode one and episode two on the Tasmania Evenings program page. Credits: Carriage
